pages.raw_text:
The initiative and referendum
forces have a candidate who quail
fies according to their standard in
William Matthews, Spokane, run
ming for the legislature in the Sixth
district. In announcing his plat
form Mr. Matthews says:


81 stand squarely upon the state
and national democratic platforms.


81 have pledged my support to
the candidate for the u s. senate
receiving the strongest endorse
ment at my party primaries.


81 am for the initiative and refer
endum and all that is included in
the term direct legislation. be-
lieve the people must learn to think
more on polictical and economic
questions and depend less upon
their representatives to perform
this important function.


"While the people may and will
make mistakes, experience is dem
onstrating that they are less liable
to pass on Objectionable laws than
are our legislatures.


while do not believe in great
err restriction as remedy for the
evils and abuses of the liquor traf-
fic | am willing to submit the prop
ositon of prohibition to the people
for their acceptance or rejection.


81 believe in an equitable system
of taxation than will fall in pro-
portion to the material benefits be-
slowed by the state. Any reform
in taxation must proceed in the d.
rection of relieving the settler,
home builder and general indus
try; and correspondinkly increase
the burden upon monopolies and
special privileges. I therefore ia
vor the constitutional tax amend
ment, for only by ratifying this
amendment at the fall election will
it be possible to make any change
in our present system.


81 believe in several particulars
our primary law is meeting with
popular disfavor and that it should
be amended accordingly.


Should the people desire me to
represent them in Olympia l will
do everything in my power to
further the interests of the Inland
Empire and of Spokane and the
Sixth legislative district in parti-
cular."
